text:
Once upon an era, there was a peculiar clock that sang.

Every morning, just as dawn filled the sky, its song would resound through the town. Each note was different, echoing with wisdom and tales untold.

There stood an old man named Hilda, whose ears could pick up these songs effortlessly. Birds halted their chirping; fishes paused their swimming; even the breeze seemed to slow.

Hilda lived next door to this magical clock. Though old, she had ears sharp enough to catch the slightest melody.

"Ah," she sighed," that nightjar hasn't begun yet."

With her granddaughter Lena joining her, they plotted a visit.

"You know the clock!" Lena exclaimed. "I can hear every tune it has sung."

They approached the brass shell that housed the clock's singing voice. Lena placed her cheek against it.

"I'll close my eyes," she murmured, and sure enough, just like hearing a whispered tale, each song filled her mind.

On a whimsical
